mainEntity.name: How much does a Jaguar S-Type 4.0 V8 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned AJ-V8 4.0-litre engine typically costs £4,500–£7,500 supply only. Used engines are available from £2,500–£4,500. Fitting adds £1,200–£1,800 depending on your location and workshop. Main dealer new engines are no longer available — the AJ-V8 4.0 is out of production. Most Jaguar engine replacements range from £3,000 to £9,000 depending on the model and engine type.

mainEntity.name: Is the Jaguar S-Type 4.0 V8 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The AJ-V8 is generally considered a stout and reliable motor. However, it has known weak points: timing chain tensioners (upgrade to metal), Nikasil liner issues on early engines (pre-1999), and cooling system components. With proper maintenance, these engines can be very reliable — but they demand attention and budget.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Jaguar S-Type 4.0 V8?

acceptedAnswer.text: The most frequently reported issues are timing chain tensioner wear (rattling on cold start), Nikasil cylinder liner deterioration on pre-1999 engines, coolant crossover pipe corrosion, oil leaks from valve cover gaskets and VVT seals, water pump failure, and throttle body issues. These are all well-understood and fixable.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Jaguar S-Type 4.0 V8 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and condition. Against a well-kept £6,000 S-Type 4.0 V8, a £6,000 recon is 100% — approaching our 60% Jaguar threshold. Against a tired £3,000 example, the same recon significantly exceeds the threshold — get a scrap valuation first. Well-maintained S-Types are becoming rarer — a proper restoration is often justified for a clean example.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Jaguar S-Type 4.0 V8 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The S-Type 4.0 V8 carries the engine code AJ-V8 (also referred to as AJ26 or AJ27 in some documentation). It's a 4.0-litre naturally aspirated V8 petrol engine used in the S-Type X200 from 1999 to 2002. The badge on the tailgate is the easiest tell — '4.0 V8'.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the timing chain tensioners, or does it need full engine replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative upgrade to metal timing chain tensioners costs £200–£1,000 for the upper tensioners (DIY-friendly). Early plastic tensioners can crack and fail. The AJ series engines are of a zero-clearance (interference) design — if a tensioner fails and the chain breaks, pistons will strike valves, requiring cylinder head removal and potentially engine replacement. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. Regular oil changes are the single most effective preventative measure.

replacementCosts.h2: Jaguar S-Type 4.0 V8 Engine Replacement Costs

rows.bestFor: No longer available from Jaguar — the AJ-V8 4.0 is out of production

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the AJ-V8 4.0-litre petrol engine. Most Jaguar engine replacements range from £3,000 to £9,000 depending on the model and engine type. Dealer quotes for Jaguar V8 engine replacements can be around £15,000–£30,000. Reconditioned engines are available in the £8,000–£10,000 range. Used AJ26 4.0L V8 engines from various builders start at around $2,750 (approximately £2,200).
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting an S-Type 4.0 V8 engine typically takes 14–18 hours at £70–£100/hr (independent) or £120–£180/hr (London/SE dealer).

cta.label: → Compare S-Type 4.0 V8 engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure — AJ26/AJ27 V8
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start, metallic ticking from the engine, engine management light, eventual rough running or complete failure to start. A loose timing chain tensioner will produce a clearly audible rattle.
problems.replacementCost: £4,500–£8,500 (if chain breaks and valves strike pistons)

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today

problems.recommendation: The AJ-V8 uses four timing chains and four tensioners. The upper tensioners are more prone to failure and are easier to replace. Early plastic tensioners can crack, causing the secondary chain to become slack. The AJ series engines are of a zero-clearance (interference) design — if a tensioner fails and the chain breaks, pistons will strike valves, requiring cylinder head removal and potentially engine replacement. A preventative upgrade to the latest metal-type tensioners is essential. Listen to the engine before turning up the radio — if you hear a rattle, have it inspected immediately.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide

problems.title: Nikasil Cylinder Liner Deterioration — Early AJ26 (pre-1999)
problems.symptoms: Difficult cold starting, lumpy idle, loss of compression, misfire, excessive oil consumption.
problems.replacementCost: £4,500–£8,500 (if cylinder bore damage is too severe)

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear

problems.recommendation: Early AJ-V8 engines (up to mid-1999) used Nikasil-coated cylinder liners. High-sulphur fuel reacted with the Nikasil coating, causing engine failures. Jaguar replaced affected engines with conventional cast-iron liners. High-sulphur fuel is no longer used in the UK, so if an original Nikasil engine has survived this long, it's likely fine. However, if you're buying a pre-1999 S-Type 4.0 V8, check for a tag on the back of the engine block indicating whether it was replaced with a steel-lined unit. A compression or blow-by test can confirm cylinder health.

failureLink.label: Read the full Nikasil failure guide

problems.title: Coolant Crossover Pipe Corrosion & Cooling System Leaks
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss with no visible external leak, overheating, sweet smell from the engine bay, low coolant warning light.
problems.replacementCost: £4,500–£8,500 (if overheating leads to warped heads or blown gaskets)
problems.recommendation: Coolant crossover pipes are a known weakness on the AJ-V8. These aluminium parts can crack or corrode, leading to coolant leaks and overheating. Ignoring coolant leaks can result in warped heads or blown gaskets — repairs that can cost well over £3,000. If you're experiencing unexplained coolant loss, have the cooling system inspected promptly.

failureLink.label: Read the full coolant leak guide

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: S-Type 4.0 V8 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — upgrade to metal tensioners before failure
If caught early, steel liners can be fitted; often more cost-effective to replace the engine
Standard maintenance on ageing V8 engines
Typically requires full engine replacement or complete rebuild

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 4.0 V8? Check These First
buyIf: * Post-1999 build (steel cylinder liners, not Nikasil)
* Timing chain tensioners upgraded to metal type
* Cooling system service records (crossover pipes, water pump replaced)
* Full service history with evidence of regular oil changes — these engines are sensitive to oil quality
avoidIf: * Cold-start rattle with no timing chain history
* Pre-1999 build with no proof of Nikasil liner replacement or health check
* Coolant loss with no documented cooling system work
* Poor service history — the AJ-V8 is reliable but needs good maintenance

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The S-Type 4.0 V8 was the performance flagship of the X200 range at launch, powered by Jaguar's all-aluminium AJ-V8. It's a smooth, refined, and characterful engine that's generally stout and reliable. However, it has known weak points: timing chain tensioners (upgrade to metal), Nikasil liner issues on early engines (pre-1999), and cooling system components. These are well-understood and fixable issues, but they demand attention and budget. Buy on documented history, not on faith. A well-maintained S-Type 4.0 V8 is a £3,000–£8,000 future classic; a neglected one is a £5,000 engine replacement waiting to happen.

cta.label: → Compare S-Type 4.0 V8 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Jaguar S-Type 4.0 V8 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattling or ticking (timing chain tensioner warning)
* Service History — including the AJ-V8 era (1999–2002) and confirmed oil-change intervals — these engines are sensitive to oil quality
* Nikasil Check (pre-1999) — Check for a tag on the back of the engine block indicating steel liners; a compression or blow-by test can confirm cylinder health
* Coolant Check — Any history of coolant loss or crossover pipe/water pump replacement?
* Smoke Check — White smoke on startup can indicate coolant ingress
* Oil Leak Check — Any evidence of oil leaks around the valve covers or VVT seals?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved timing, cooling, or sensor issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
